description: The workflow runs with the default write-all GITHUB_TOKEN, giving every job and dependency push and admin reach.
body: Failure mode: no permissions block is declared, so GITHUB_TOKEN keeps the repository default (often write-all), and any compromised step or malicious dependency can push commits, alter releases, or comment as the bot across the repo. Detection signals: no top-level or per-job permissions:; jobs that only test still hold contents:write; an injected step can push to branches. Do not rely on the default token scope. Safer fallback: set a top-level permissions: contents: read and grant the minimal write scope only on the specific job that needs it, then verify the token cannot reach protected branches.
